directive: Make exactly two changes: (1) Replace the bucket array with Prometheus's published defaults — [0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10] — adding the missing 5ms bucket. (2) Add a JSDoc comment on the middleware function in src/middleware/metrics.ts stating 'Express only: relies on req.route.path for normalized route labels; on other frameworks req.route is undefined and route label will be unknown.' Update tests/middleware/metrics.test.ts to assert the 0.005 bucket boundary is present.
rationale: Both Round 2 rejection concerns are concrete and fixable without spec amendment or operator escalation. Concern 1 (bucket boundaries): the phase spec says 'per Prometheus best practices' — the Executor chose buckets starting at 0.01 (10ms), but Prometheus's published default histogram buckets start at 0.005 (5ms): [0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10]. The Reviewer's remark that '2.5 is not in defaults' is incorrect — Prometheus defaults do include 2.5 — but the missing 0.005 bucket is a genuine deviation. Aligning to the published defaults satisfies the spec's 'best practices' language. Concern 2 (framework scope): the phase spec does not say 'framework-agnostic'; scoping to Express and documenting the limitation is the minimal path that satisfies the Reviewer's option (a) without expanding scope beyond what the spec requires.
